senses.gloss: passive participle of نَشَرَ (našara); published, issued, announced
passive participle of نَشَرَ (našara); published, issued, announced; posted (on facebook, etc)

etymology.text: Derived from the passive participle of the verb نَشَرَ (našara). In the meaning of prism compare Hebrew מִנְסָרָה (minsará, “prism”) and Assyrian Neo-Aramaic ܢܣܝܼܪܵܐ (nsīrā, “prism”). Possibly Calque of Ancient Greek πρίσμα (prísma, “something sawed”).
